title: Serving God, Saving the King
summary: “Now when Daniel knew that the writing was signed, he went into his house; and his windows being open in his chamber toward Jerusalem, he kneeled upon his knees three times a day, and prayed, and gave thanks before his God, as he did aforetime.”(Daniel 6:10). Daniel had been promoted. No one was more powerful than him in the Kingdom of Babylon except King Darius. Promotion and blessing brings envy also. The other Babylonian princes were not just happy to bring dishonor to Daniel, they wanted to destroy him completely. The reason is twofold: Daniel was appointed over them not just in position, but to ensure everyone paid their taxes and dispensed of their responsibilities. “And over these three presidents; of whom Daniel was first: that the princes might give accounts unto them, and the king should have no damage” (Daniel 6:2). Despite his exalted stature, and old age (he was over 80 years old), he would kneel down three times a day to pray to the God. Kneeling down is the posture of a beggar. It was begging God that always blessed and delivered Daniel from the capricious heathen Kings of Babylon. The scripture states that the “writing was signed”. In other words, praying to the God of Israel was now a state crime punishable by death. What can give a person the courage to accept the death sentence? Why will a man who was President of Babylon throw away everything to become lion food?
